---
title: "USDA_DB"
output: html_document
author: "Ozge Shera Kuddar"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(reticulate)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(engine = "python", python.reticulate.autoprint = FALSE)
```

# 1. Donwloaded each state data (https://plants.usda.gov/home/stateSearch)
# 2. Combine and keep the unique entries (All_States_USDA_data.csv)
# 3. Generate binomial scientific name (All_States_USDA_Scientific_Name.csv)

```{r, engine='python'} 
import pandas as pd
import glob

# Define the path to the CSV files
path = "./USDA_DB/State_csv_files/*.csv"

# Define the header columns for the final CSV
final_columns = ["Accepted Symbol", "Scientific Name", "Common Name"]

# Initialize an empty DataFrame for storing unique rows
final_df = pd.DataFrame(columns=final_columns)

# Loop through each CSV file
for file in glob.glob(path):
    # Read the CSV file, skipping the first two rows, and selecting only the required columns
    df = pd.read_csv(file, skiprows=2, usecols=[0, 2, 3], header=None)
    df.columns = final_columns

    # Append the unique rows to the final DataFrame
    final_df = pd.concat([final_df, df]).drop_duplicates()

# Write the final DataFrame to a CSV file
final_df.to_csv("./USDA_DB/All_States_USDA_data.csv", index=False)

# Function to process the Scientific Name column
def process_scientific_name(name):
    # Add a space after "×" if not already present
    name = name.replace("×", "x ")
    
    # Split the name into words
    words = name.split()

    # Keep only the first 2 words, or 3 if the second word is "×"
    if len(words) > 2 and words[1] == "x":
        return " ".join(words[:3])
    else:
        return " ".join(words[:2])

# Apply the function to the Scientific Name column
final_df['Scientific Name'] = final_df['Scientific Name'].apply(process_scientific_name)

# Remove duplicate species names to ensure uniqueness
final_scientific_name_df = final_df[['Scientific Name']].drop_duplicates()

# Write the new DataFrame with unique Scientific Names to a CSV file
final_scientific_name_df.to_csv("./USDA_DB/All_States_USDA_Scientific_Name.csv", index=False)

```


# Get the taxid and taxonomy (R) 

```{R}

# Load the necessary library
library(taxizedb)

# Read the CSV file containing only the Scientific Name column
scientific_names <- read.csv("./USDA_DB/All_States_USDA_Scientific_Name.csv")

# Define the path to your local NCBI taxdump files
taxdump_path <- "./taxdumpAug2024.tar.gz" # Download the taxdump file of your choice

a <- as.data.frame(name2taxid(scientific_names$Scientific.Name, db = "ncbi", path = taxdump_path, out_type = 'summary'))
duplicates <- a[duplicated(a$name) | duplicated(a$name, fromLast = TRUE), ]

# Identify which Scientific.Name are missing in the 'a' dataframe
missing_names <- scientific_names[!(scientific_names$Scientific.Name %in% a$name), ]

# Save the new dataframe with the missing names to a text file
write.table(missing_names, "./USDA_DB/Missing_Scientific_Names.txt", row.names = FALSE, col.names = FALSE, quote = FALSE)

missing_names <- as.data.frame(missing_names)
first_word_df <- data.frame(First.Word = unique(sapply(strsplit(missing_names$missing_names, " "), `[`, 1)))
b <- as.data.frame(name2taxid(first_word_df$First.Word, db = "ncbi", path = taxdump_path, out_type = 'summary'))
duplicates <- b[duplicated(b$name) | duplicated(b$name, fromLast = TRUE), ]

# Rename the columns
colnames(a) <- c("Species", "TaxID")

# Save the dataframe as a CSV file
write.csv(a, "./USDA_DB/Species_TaxID.csv", row.names = FALSE)



# Create taxonomy

# Assume your dataframe is named 'a' and has a column 'TaxID'
# Initialize an empty list to store taxonomy information
taxonomy_info <- list()

# Loop through each TaxID in the dataframe and get the classification
for (i in 1:nrow(a)) {
  taxid <- a$TaxID[i]
  
  # Get the classification for the given TaxID
  classification_result <- classification(taxid, db = "ncbi")
  
  # Initialize variables for each rank
  kingdom <- NA
  phylum <- NA
  class <- NA
  order <- NA
  family <- NA
  genus <- NA
  species <- NA
  
  # Check if the classification_result is not NULL and assign ranks
  if (!is.null(classification_result)) {
    if ("kingdom" %in% classification_result[[1]]$rank) {
      kingdom <- classification_result[[1]]$name[classification_result[[1]]$rank == "kingdom"]
    }
    if ("phylum" %in% classification_result[[1]]$rank) {
      phylum <- classification_result[[1]]$name[classification_result[[1]]$rank == "phylum"]
    }
    if ("class" %in% classification_result[[1]]$rank) {
      class <- classification_result[[1]]$name[classification_result[[1]]$rank == "class"]
    }
    if ("order" %in% classification_result[[1]]$rank) {
      order <- classification_result[[1]]$name[classification_result[[1]]$rank == "order"]
    }
    if ("family" %in% classification_result[[1]]$rank) {
      family <- classification_result[[1]]$name[classification_result[[1]]$rank == "family"]
    }
    if ("genus" %in% classification_result[[1]]$rank) {
      genus <- classification_result[[1]]$name[classification_result[[1]]$rank == "genus"]
    }
    if ("species" %in% classification_result[[1]]$rank) {
      species <- classification_result[[1]]$name[classification_result[[1]]$rank == "species"]
    }
  }
  
  # Store the taxonomy information in the list
  taxonomy_info[[i]] <- data.frame(
    TaxID = taxid,
    Kingdom = kingdom,
    Phylum = phylum,
    Class = class,
    Order = order,
    Family = family,
    Genus = genus,
    Species = species
  )
}

# Combine the taxonomy information into a single dataframe
taxonomy_df <- do.call(rbind, taxonomy_info)

# Save the taxonomy information to a CSV file
write.csv(taxonomy_df, "./USDA_DB/USDA_Taxonomy_Only_for_Species_exist.csv", row.names = FALSE)


```
